top_signals.title: Oil Prices Top $90 as Kuwaiti Tanker Hit in Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: A Kuwait-owned oil products tanker was struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman as tensions escalate around the key shipping lanes of the Middle East, sending Brent back above $90 per barrel.  The Kaifan tanker, owned by Kuwait Oil Tanker Co. S.A.K., was the vessel hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait, per security consultancy EOS Risk Group cited by Bloomberg. The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations UKMTO said early on Tuesday that it had received a report of an incident 8 nautical miles northeast of Limah, Oman.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: As the U.S.-Iran war heats up again, these parts of the stock market and economy could be affected
top_signals.summary: Investors and economists are deliberating what the latest exchange in hostilities will mean for the broader economy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Russian parliament passes law regulating crypto market
top_signals.summary: The State Duma legislation creates a regulated framework for crypto market participants and now moves to President Vladimir Putin for his signature before becoming law.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
